description_long: This object consists of a group of medicine bottles and vials, each with a distinct shape and size, arranged in a row. The bottles are primarily cylindrical with rounded shoulders and narrow necks, topped with dark caps. Some bottles have attached tags or labels, which are rectangular and hang from the neck or are affixed to the front. The glass or plastic bodies of the bottles are tinted a muted pinkish hue, while the caps and labels are darker, providing contrast. The labels feature visible text and some have additional decorative elements, such as borders or emblems. The overall appearance suggests a collection of pharmaceutical containers, likely used for liquid medicines or tinctures, suitable for placement in an urban pharmacy, clinic, or first aid station.
description: A group of pink-tinted medicine bottles and vials with dark caps and labeled tags, arranged in a row. The containers vary in size and shape, with visible text and decorative label elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing all bottles and vials lined up, with labels and tags clearly visible on the front faces.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth and cylindrical profiles of the bottles, with tags and labels seen edge-on.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the bottles, with less visible labeling and more focus on the bottle shapes.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the bottles' profiles and the attachment points of tags and labels.
